options.C: The determinant of a matrix formed by the vectors

options.B: Convert images to HSV color space only

questions.explanation: train() activates training behavior (e.g., dropout, BatchNorm updates); eval() uses inference behavior.

options.A: train(): enables dropout/BatchNorm updates; eval(): disables dropout and uses running BatchNorm stats
options.B: train(): freezes all weights; eval(): unfreezes all weights
options.C: train(): switches to CPU; eval(): switches to GPU
options.D: train(): computes gradients; eval(): permanently disables autograd for the model

questions.explanation: Convolutions reuse the same kernel across locations, drastically reducing parameters and leveraging local structure.

options.A: They reduce parameters and exploit spatial locality/translation equivariance
options.B: They guarantee perfect rotation invariance without augmentation
options.C: They eliminate the need for nonlinear activations
options.D: They always outperform transformers regardless of data size

questions.question: Why do CNNs typically use weight sharing and local receptive fields (convolutions) instead of fully connected layers for images?

questions.explanation: Too-large learning rates commonly cause oscillation/divergence; lowering LR or scheduling it improves stability.

options.B: Increase image resolution as much as possible
options.D: Switch the loss function to accuracy

options.C: Percentage of images with at least one detection
options.D: Area under the ROC curve computed from pixel-wise probabilities
